meta.notes: V1 Knowledge Graph for Permit Whisperer. Sources: sftreasurer.org, sf.gov, sfplanning.org, sfdph.org, sf-fire.org, sfgov.org/entertainment, sanfranciscopolice.org, californiaregisteredagent.com, asnanicpa.com, sffta.org, California Secretary of State. All fees and requirements verified against 2025-2026 published schedules. V0.2 adds: music licensing (PRO), ADA/accessibility compliance, SF employer ordinances (HCSO, PSLO, PPLO, Fair Chance, Commuter Benefits), waste/recycling compliance, tobacco restrictions, CASp inspection requirements, Accessible Barrier Removal Grant, and automation flags for each step.

regulatory_agencies.name: Office of the Treasurer & Tax Collector (TTX)
regulatory_agencies.role: Business registration, tax collection, unified license fee billing
regulatory_agencies.address: City Hall, Room 140, 1 Carlton B. Goodlett Place, SF 94102
regulatory_agencies.notes: First Year Free program waives initial registration fee for many businesses. Registration now required within 30 days of commencing business. BAN (Business Account Number) needed for all other permits.

regulatory_agencies.name: Department of Public Health (DPH) – Environmental Health Branch
regulatory_agencies.role: Health permits for food facilities, body art, pools, hazardous materials
regulatory_agencies.address: 49 South Van Ness Ave, Suite 600, SF 94103
regulatory_agencies.notes: Online food application available at etaxstatement.sfgov.org/dphehbfoodpermit. Site evaluation available before signing a lease — highly recommended.

regulatory_agencies.name: SF Planning Department
regulatory_agencies.role: Zoning compliance, use permits, conditional use authorization, formula retail
regulatory_agencies.address: 49 South Van Ness Ave, 2nd Floor (Permit Center), SF 94103
regulatory_agencies.notes: As of Feb 2026, Permit Center only accepts online applications for in-kind replacements. Walk-ins for consultations on first-come basis. Office of Small Business offers permitting support. Check zoning BEFORE signing a lease.

regulatory_agencies.name: Department of Building Inspection (DBI)
regulatory_agencies.role: Building permits, tenant improvement permits, occupancy certificates
regulatory_agencies.notes: Required for any physical modifications to a space. Even minor changes (new walls, plumbing for food service, ADA modifications) require permits. PermitSF online portal launched Feb 2026 for some permit types.

regulatory_agencies.name: San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD) – Fire Prevention
regulatory_agencies.role: Fire permits, occupancy load, fire safety inspections, hazardous materials
regulatory_agencies.notes: Required for all places of assembly, restaurants, bars, and any business storing hazardous materials. Must be cleared before DPH will issue food permit.

regulatory_agencies.name: San Francisco Police Department (SFPD)
regulatory_agencies.role: Permits for secondhand dealers, massage establishments, billiard halls, amusement devices
regulatory_agencies.notes: Background checks required for most SFPD permits. Processing times vary significantly.

regulatory_agencies.name: Entertainment Commission
regulatory_agencies.role: Entertainment permits for live music, DJs, dancing, extended hours
regulatory_agencies.address: 49 South Van Ness Ave, Suite 1240, SF 94103
regulatory_agencies.notes: POE permits required for regular live entertainment. LLP is a streamlined option for smaller venues. Neighborhood notification requirements may apply.

regulatory_agencies.name: California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control (ABC)
regulatory_agencies.role: Liquor licenses for on-premise and off-premise alcohol sales
regulatory_agencies.address: 1515 Clay St, Suite 2206, Oakland CA 94612 (nearest office)
regulatory_agencies.notes: STATE agency, not city. License transfers are common and faster than new licenses. Type 47 is most common for restaurants. Census tract limits may apply. Costs range from $1,000-$15,000+ for new licenses; transfers can cost $50K-$300K+ depending on type and location.

regulatory_agencies.name: Department of Public Works (DPW) / SF Public Works
regulatory_agencies.role: Sidewalk permits, street space, awnings, outdoor seating, food truck routes
regulatory_agencies.notes: Shared Spaces program (formerly temporary COVID parklets) has been made permanent. Application through sfpublicworks.org.

regulatory_agencies.name: Animal Care and Control (ACC)
regulatory_agencies.role: Dog walker permits, pet-related business permits
regulatory_agencies.notes: Commercial dog walkers limited to max 8 dogs at a time. Background check and insurance required.

regulatory_agencies.name: Office of Cannabis
key_actions: Cannabis business permit (retail, delivery, manufacturing, distribution, testing)
regulatory_agencies.notes: Cannabis Business Tax effective Jan 1, 2026 (1-5% of gross receipts). Requires both city permit and state license from DCC. Equity applicants receive priority.

regulatory_agencies.name: California Secretary of State
regulatory_agencies.notes: Must register entity with state BEFORE registering business with SF TTX. Can file online. Fictitious Business Name filed with SF County Clerk.

regulatory_agencies.name: SF Ethics Commission
regulatory_agencies.role: Permit consultant registration (relevant for OUR business)
regulatory_agencies.notes: CRITICAL FOR US: Anyone providing permit consulting services for compensation MUST register within 5 business days. Quarterly reports required. This is our legal requirement and competitive moat.

regulatory_agencies.name: Office of Labor Standards Enforcement (OLSE)
regulatory_agencies.role: Enforces SF employment ordinances: minimum wage, paid sick leave, health care security, paid parental leave, Fair Chance, commuter benefits
regulatory_agencies.address: City Hall, 1 Dr Carlton B. Goodlett Place, SF 94102
key_actions: Minimum Wage Ordinance compliance ($18.67/hr as of 2024, adjusted annually)
regulatory_agencies.notes: SF has MORE employer requirements than any other US city. Layered on top of California state law. Minimum wage is adjusted annually by CPI. HCSO applies to employers with 20+ employees worldwide ($2.56-$3.85/hr per employee for healthcare in 2026). PSLO: 1 hr per 30 hrs worked, caps at 40 hrs (<10 employees) or 72 hrs (10+ employees).

regulatory_agencies.name: Performing Rights Organizations (ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, GMR)
regulatory_agencies.role: Music licensing for public performance of copyrighted music
regulatory_agencies.notes: CRITICAL: Any business playing music publicly — even background Spotify (which is NOT licensed for commercial use) — needs PRO licenses. Only exemption: establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing conventional radio/TV with 6 or fewer speakers and no cover charge. PROs actively send auditors to businesses. Fines: $750 per song per infringement. Annual cost for all 3: ~$400-$700 for a typical small restaurant/retail. Alternative: use a B2B music service (Rockbot, Soundtrack, Soundsuit) that bundles PRO licensing.

regulatory_agencies.name: SF Environment (Dept of Environment)
regulatory_agencies.role: Waste reduction, recycling, composting mandates, environmental compliance
key_actions: Mandatory Recycling and Composting Ordinance compliance
regulatory_agencies.notes: SF requires ALL businesses to separate waste into 3 streams: recycling, composting, landfill. Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers. No polystyrene foam. Free technical assistance available from SF Environment for setup.

regulatory_agencies.name: Certified Access Specialist (CASp) Program
regulatory_agencies.role: ADA and California accessibility compliance inspections
key_actions: Accessible Barrier Removal Grant application (up to $10,000 from SF Office of Small Business)
regulatory_agencies.notes: CRITICAL: SF is a hotspot for serial ADA lawsuits. California's Unruh Act allows $4,000+ per violation plus attorney fees. DBI does NOT conduct general ADA review during building permit sign-off — that's YOUR responsibility. CASp inspection provides legal benefits: 90-day stay on lawsuits + mandatory early evaluation conference if sued. SF grants up to $10,000 for CASp inspections and improvements.

universal_requirements.description: Every business operating in San Francisco must complete these steps, regardless of business type.

steps.name: Choose business structure
steps.detail: Decide: sole proprietorship, LLC, corporation, partnership. Most small businesses choose LLC for liability protection. File with CA Secretary of State if forming an entity.

automation.what_claude_does: Generate comparison analysis of LLC vs Corp vs Sole Prop for client's specific situation. Pre-fill Articles of Organization. Generate operating agreement template.
automation.what_nathe_does: Review recommendation with client. Client signs and submits.

steps.name: Obtain EIN (Employer Identification Number)
steps.detail: Required for all entities except single-member sole proprietorships with no employees. Free from IRS.gov.

automation.what_claude_does: Walk client through IRS online application step-by-step. Can pre-fill all fields based on intake data. Generate the application answers.
automation.what_nathe_does: Client submits on IRS.gov (cannot be done by third party).

steps.name: Verify zoning compliance
steps.detail: BEFORE signing a lease or committing to a location, verify that your intended use is Permitted (P) or Conditionally Permitted (C) in that zoning district. If Not Permitted (NP), you cannot operate there. Use SF Planning's Property Information Map.
steps.cost: $0 for self-research / varies for formal determination
steps.timeline: Same day for self-research; weeks for formal letter

automation.what_claude_does: Look up zoning district for client's address using SF Planning Property Information Map. Cross-reference intended use against permitted/conditional/not-permitted uses for that district. Generate zoning compliance memo.
automation.what_nathe_does: Visit Permit Center to confirm if needed. Handle any Change of Use application.

steps.name: Register business with SF TTX
steps.detail: Obtain Business Registration Certificate. Required within 30 days of commencing business. Apply online. First Year Free program may waive fee. You receive a Business Account Number (BAN) which is required for all other SF permits.
steps.cost: $0 (First Year Free for qualifying) to $35,002 based on gross receipts + $4 state fee
steps.timeline: 10-15 business days for certificate after submission and payment

automation.what_claude_does: Pre-fill the online registration form with all client data. Calculate estimated registration fee. Determine First Year Free eligibility. Generate step-by-step submission guide with screenshots.
automation.what_nathe_does: Client reviews and submits (DocuSign required). Payment processing.

steps.name: Obtain California Seller's Permit (if selling tangible goods)
steps.detail: Required if selling physical products. Apply through California Department of Tax and Fee Administration (CDTFA). Free. Allows you to collect sales tax (SF rate: 8.625% in 2026).

steps.name: Fictitious Business Name (DBA) if applicable
steps.detail: Required if operating under a name different from your legal entity name. File with SF County Clerk-Recorder's office. Must also publish in a local newspaper.

business_type_profiles.name: Restaurant / Café / Food Service
business_type_profiles.description: Brick-and-mortar establishment preparing and serving food to the public

permits_required.notes: If location was not previously a restaurant, a Change of Use permit may be required. Formula retail (11+ locations) triggers Conditional Use Authorization.
Required for any buildout — kitchen equipment, plumbing, ventilation, ADA modifications. Must hire licensed architect/contractor.
Requires plan check ($), application fee, food safety manager certification, final inspection. Can request site evaluation BEFORE leasing. Online application available.
Fire clearance required before DPH issues health permit. Includes occupancy load, fire suppression system (hood system for cooking), exit signage, fire extinguishers.
For outdoor dining. Shared Spaces program made permanent. Application through SF Public Works.
Type 41 (Beer/Wine, Eating Place) or Type 47 (Full Bar, Eating Place). License transfers from previous occupant common. New licenses: $1,000-$15,000; transfers: $50K-$300K+.
Required if offering live music, DJs, or dancing. Limited Live Performance permit for low-key entertainment.

additional_requirements: Food Safety Manager Certification (at least 1 staff member)
Food Handler Cards for all food-handling employees
Workers' Compensation Insurance (if employees)
General Liability Insurance
Grease trap installation (plumbing permit required)
ADA compliance verification
Menu/signage compliance with SF health disclosure requirements

Music licensing: PRO licenses from ASCAP (~$150-400/yr), BMI (~$150-250/yr), and SESAC (~$100-150/yr) required if playing ANY music publicly. Exemption only for establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing radio with 6 or fewer speakers. Alternative: use B2B music service (~$30-60/mo) that bundles licensing.

ADA/Accessibility: CASp inspection strongly recommended ($500-$2,000) — provides legal protection against serial litigants. Apply for Accessible Barrier Removal Grant (up to $10,000) from Office of Small Business. SF is a hotspot for ADA lawsuits under Unruh Act ($4,000+ per violation).

Waste compliance: SF mandatory 3-stream waste separation (recycling, composting, landfill). Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers. No polystyrene. No free checkout bags ($0.25+ charge required). Free setup assistance from SF Environment.

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Signing a lease before verifying zoning — this is the #1 mistake
Not requesting a DPH site evaluation before committing to a space
Underestimating buildout permit timeline (DBI can take months)
Not budgeting for liquor license transfer costs
Forgetting about grease interceptor requirements
Not registering for Gross Receipts Tax in addition to registration

business_type_profiles.name: Retail Store (Non-Food)
business_type_profiles.description: Physical retail location selling goods to the public

permits_required.notes: Most retail uses are permitted in commercial zones. Formula retail (11+ locations worldwide) requires CUA hearing. Check neighborhood-specific restrictions.
Only if making physical modifications. Minor cosmetic changes may not require permits.
Required for collecting sales tax on tangible goods.
Required for new exterior signage. Planning Code Article 6 governs signs. Some historic districts have additional restrictions.

additional_requirements: ADA compliance
Weights and Measures registration (if using scales)
Secondhand Dealer Permit from SFPD (if selling used goods)
Tobacco Retailer License from DPH (if selling tobacco — note SF ban on flavored tobacco)

Music licensing: PRO licenses from ASCAP (~$150-400/yr), BMI (~$150-250/yr), and SESAC (~$100-150/yr) required if playing ANY music publicly. Exemption only for establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing radio with 6 or fewer speakers. Alternative: use B2B music service (~$30-60/mo) that bundles licensing.

ADA/Accessibility: CASp inspection strongly recommended ($500-$2,000) — provides legal protection against serial litigants. Apply for Accessible Barrier Removal Grant (up to $10,000) from Office of Small Business. SF is a hotspot for ADA lawsuits under Unruh Act ($4,000+ per violation).

Waste compliance: SF mandatory 3-stream waste separation (recycling, composting, landfill). Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers. No polystyrene. No free checkout bags ($0.25+ charge required). Free setup assistance from SF Environment.

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Formula retail trap — if you or your franchisor have 11+ locations worldwide, you need CUA
Not checking for neighborhood-specific use restrictions
Signage violations — SF sign code is unusually strict
Not registering for Commercial Rents Tax if applicable

business_type_profiles.name: Food Truck / Mobile Food Facility
business_type_profiles.description: Vehicle-based food service operating on public or private property

permits_required.notes: 5 categories of MFF. Requires plan check, commissary verification, restroom verification, final inspection. Must have approved commissary kitchen.
Fire safety inspection of truck, propane/gas systems, fire extinguisher, suppression hood.

permits_required.permit: Public Property Route Approval (if operating on public streets)
permits_required.notes: Buffer rules: 75ft from restaurants, 500ft from elementary schools, 1,000ft from high schools during hours.

permits_required.permit: Temporary Use Authorization (if operating on private property)
permits_required.notes: Fees start at $500. Organizations like Off the Grid manage permitted sites (charge 10-20% of sales).

additional_requirements: Commissary kitchen agreement
DMV vehicle registration
Vehicle insurance
Workers' Compensation Insurance (if employees)
General Liability Insurance ($2M)
Food Safety Manager Certification
Seller's Permit from CDTFA

Waste compliance: SF mandatory 3-stream waste separation (recycling, composting, landfill). Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers. No polystyrene. No free checkout bags ($0.25+ charge required). Free setup assistance from SF Environment.

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Not securing a commissary agreement before applying
Underestimating buffer zone restrictions — significantly limits operating locations
Not budgeting for Off the Grid or similar platform fees
Late license renewal penalties (10-50%)

business_type_profiles.name: Home-Based Business
business_type_profiles.description: Business operated from a residential address (consulting, e-commerce, freelancing, cottage food)

permits_required.notes: Yes, even home-based businesses must register with TTX. First Year Free program often applies.

permits_required.permit: Cottage Food Operation Permit (if making food at home)
permits_required.notes: California Homemade Food Act allows certain foods to be made and sold from home. Class A (direct sales only) vs Class B (sell to third parties like cafes). Class B requires kitchen inspection.

additional_requirements: SF does NOT require a Home Occupancy Permit (unlike many cities)
Must follow rules: no employees at home, no customer visits generating traffic, no signage, no inventory that creates hazard
Seller's Permit from CDTFA if selling goods
Consider LLC formation for liability protection
common_pitfalls: Thinking you don't need to register because you work from home — you do
Cottage food operators: Class B requires DPH inspection and has $75K annual revenue cap
Landlord/HOA restrictions on home businesses

business_type_profiles.name: Bar / Nightclub / Lounge
business_type_profiles.description: Establishment primarily serving alcohol, potentially with entertainment

permits_required.notes: Bars often require CUA. Public hearing with Planning Commission. Neighborhood notification required. Can take 3-6 months.

Type 48 (On-Sale General, Public Premises — no food requirement) or Type 47 (requires 50%+ food sales). New licenses may not be available in over-concentrated census tracts. Transfer is often the only path.
Required for any live entertainment, DJs, dancing. Involves sound study, community input, operating conditions.

additional_requirements: Security plan may be required
Sound mitigation measures
Responsible Beverage Service (RBS) training for all servers (state requirement)
Neighborhood outreach and community meeting
ABC posting requirements (30 days public notice)

Music licensing: PRO licenses from ASCAP (~$150-400/yr), BMI (~$150-250/yr), and SESAC (~$100-150/yr) required if playing ANY music publicly. Exemption only for establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing radio with 6 or fewer speakers. Alternative: use B2B music service (~$30-60/mo) that bundles licensing.

ADA/Accessibility: CASp inspection strongly recommended ($500-$2,000) — provides legal protection against serial litigants. Apply for Accessible Barrier Removal Grant (up to $10,000) from Office of Small Business. SF is a hotspot for ADA lawsuits under Unruh Act ($4,000+ per violation).

Waste compliance: SF mandatory 3-stream waste separation (recycling, composting, landfill). Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers. No polystyrene. No free checkout bags ($0.25+ charge required). Free setup assistance from SF Environment.

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
business_type_profiles.estimated_total_cost_range: $50,000 - $300,000+ (liquor license transfer is the big cost)
common_pitfalls: Not checking census tract saturation for liquor licenses before committing to a location
Underestimating CUA timeline and neighborhood opposition risk
Not budgeting for sound engineering and mitigation
Entertainment Commission conditions can be very restrictive

business_type_profiles.name: Personal Services (Salon, Barbershop, Spa, Tattoo)

permits_required.notes: Required for tattoo/piercing establishments. Annual inspection.
Required for massage businesses. Background check for all practitioners. SFPD issues.

permits_required.permit: State Board of Barbering and Cosmetology License
permits_required.notes: State license required for all cosmetologists, barbers, estheticians, nail technicians. Establishment license also required.

additional_requirements: Individual practitioner licenses from CA Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
Establishment license from state board
Healthy Nail Salon Recognition Program (voluntary but recommended)
Workers' Comp insurance if employees

Music licensing: PRO licenses from ASCAP (~$150-400/yr), BMI (~$150-250/yr), and SESAC (~$100-150/yr) required if playing ANY music publicly. Exemption only for establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing radio with 6 or fewer speakers. Alternative: use B2B music service (~$30-60/mo) that bundles licensing.

ADA/Accessibility: CASp inspection strongly recommended ($500-$2,000) — provides legal protection against serial litigants. Apply for Accessible Barrier Removal Grant (up to $10,000) from Office of Small Business. SF is a hotspot for ADA lawsuits under Unruh Act ($4,000+ per violation).

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Not getting the state establishment license in addition to individual licenses
Massage businesses: SFPD background check can take weeks
ADA accessibility requirements for new salon buildouts

business_type_profiles.name: Professional Services / Office-Based (Consulting, Tech, Creative)
business_type_profiles.description: Office-based business not selling goods or serving food

permits_required.notes: This may be your ONLY required permit. First Year Free program likely applies.
Office use is permitted in most commercial/mixed-use zones. Verify before signing lease.

additional_requirements: Professional licenses from relevant state boards (CPA, attorney, architect, contractor, etc.)
LLC/Corp formation recommended for liability protection
Gross Receipts Tax filing (annual, based on SF-attributable revenue)
Consider E&O insurance

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Not registering at all because 'I'm just freelancing' — if you have SF income, you must register
Not filing annual Gross Receipts Tax even if tax owed is $0
Permit consultant businesses (like ours!) must also register with Ethics Commission

business_type_profiles.name: Pop-Up / Temporary Retail
business_type_profiles.description: Short-term retail or food operation, including farmer's market vendors and event booths

permits_required.notes: Required for temporary commercial use. Fees start at $500. Duration limits apply.
Required for food vendors at events. Extra fee if you don't already hold an SF food permit.

additional_requirements: Vacant to Vibrant program offers free rent + grants for downtown pop-ups (competitive application)
Tenderloin Storefront Opportunity Grant: $50K grant for 3-year leases (5 winners from 23 applicants)
Insurance typically required by landlord/event organizer
Seller's Permit from CDTFA if selling goods

Music licensing: PRO licenses from ASCAP (~$150-400/yr), BMI (~$150-250/yr), and SESAC (~$100-150/yr) required if playing ANY music publicly. Exemption only for establishments under 3,750 sq ft playing radio with 6 or fewer speakers. Alternative: use B2B music service (~$30-60/mo) that bundles licensing.

SF employer requirements (if hiring): SF minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave (1hr/30hrs worked). HCSO healthcare spending (20+ employees: $2.56-$3.85/hr). Paid Parental Leave (20+ employees). Fair Chance Ordinance (no criminal history questions until conditional offer). Commuter Benefits (20+ employees). Required workplace posters from OLSE.
common_pitfalls: Not realizing you need TTX registration even for a temporary pop-up
Missing Vacant to Vibrant application windows
Food pop-ups: underestimating DPH requirements and lead time

key_programs_and_resources.name: First Year Free
key_programs_and_resources.description: Waives first-year business registration fee for qualifying new businesses

key_programs_and_resources.name: Vacant to Vibrant
key_programs_and_resources.description: Free rent (3-6 months) + grants ($3K-$12K) for pop-ups in vacant downtown storefronts. 50% convert to long-term leases.

key_programs_and_resources.name: Tenderloin Storefront Opportunity Grant
key_programs_and_resources.description: $50,000 grant for 3-year lease in Tenderloin. Includes training and business plan development.

key_programs_and_resources.name: Office of Small Business
key_programs_and_resources.description: Free counseling, permitting navigation assistance, workshops. Overwhelmed but excellent resource.

key_programs_and_resources.name: SF SCORE Chapter
key_programs_and_resources.description: Free business mentoring from experienced volunteers. Help with business plans, financials.

key_programs_and_resources.name: La Cocina
key_programs_and_resources.description: Food business incubator specifically for women and immigrants. Provides commercial kitchen access and business support.

key_programs_and_resources.name: SF Small Business Development Center (SBDC)
key_programs_and_resources.description: Free consulting, training, and resources for small businesses.

key_programs_and_resources.name: PermitSF Portal
key_programs_and_resources.description: New online permit portal launched Feb 2026. Currently supports 5 permit types with online payment.

key_programs_and_resources.name: Accessible Barrier Removal Grant
key_programs_and_resources.description: Up to $10,000 reimbursement for CASp inspections and ADA accessibility improvements. Rolling applications. Covers equipment, fixtures, architectural services, and labor.

key_programs_and_resources.name: SF Environment Green Business Program
key_programs_and_resources.description: Free technical assistance to reduce waste, energy, and water use. Voluntary certification as a 'San Francisco Green Business' — good marketing.

sf_specific_gotchas.detail: Any business with 11+ locations worldwide (including franchisees) triggers Conditional Use Authorization — a public hearing before the Planning Commission. This adds 3-6 months and significant cost. Some neighborhoods prohibit formula retail entirely.

In addition to business registration, SF imposes a Gross Receipts Tax on all businesses. Rate varies by industry (0.053% to 0.894%). Must file annually even if no tax is owed. Separate from state income tax.
Businesses leasing commercial space in SF may owe Commercial Rents Tax on their rental expenses. Rate is 3.4% (was 1%, increased by voters). Applies to lessees, not landlords.
Additional tax on businesses with $50M+ in SF gross receipts. Likely not applicable to small businesses but worth noting.
Beginning in 2026, some license fees are waived per Prop M. Businesses may see $0 on some license line items but still must maintain the license itself.

Applies to bars/restaurants in residential or mixed-use areas. Sets operating conditions (noise, hours, etc.) that are automatically imposed. Violations can trigger community complaints and enforcement.
The pandemic-era Shared Spaces program is now permanent. Permits available through DPW. Popular but has ongoing fees and compliance requirements.
Effective Jan 1, 2026. 1-5% gross receipts tax on cannabis businesses IN ADDITION to regular Gross Receipts Tax.

ANY business playing music publicly needs licenses from ASCAP, BMI, and SESAC (and potentially GMR). This includes background music, Spotify/Pandora (NOT licensed for commercial use), live bands, DJs, karaoke, and jukeboxes. Only exemption: under 3,750 sq ft with conventional radio, 6 or fewer speakers, no cover charge. PROs actively audit businesses. Fines: $750 PER SONG. Annual cost for all 3 PROs: ~$400-$700 for typical small business. Cheaper alternative: B2B music services like Rockbot, Soundtrack Your Brand, or Soundsuit bundle PRO licensing into their subscription (~$30-60/mo).

sf_specific_gotchas.issue: ADA Compliance and Serial Litigation Risk
sf_specific_gotchas.detail: SF is a national hotspot for serial ADA litigants. California's Unruh Civil Rights Act allows minimum $4,000 per violation plus attorney fees — no proof of actual damages needed. DBI does NOT check for ADA compliance during building permit approval — that's your architect/contractor's responsibility (and ultimately yours). Get a CASp inspection ($500-$2,000) for legal protection: it triggers a 90-day stay on any lawsuit and mandatory early evaluation. SF offers Accessible Barrier Removal Grants up to $10,000.

SF has the most extensive local employment ordinances in the US, layered ON TOP of California law. Key requirements: Minimum wage $18.67/hr (adjusted annually). Paid Sick Leave: 1hr/30hrs worked, caps at 40 or 72 hrs depending on employer size. Health Care Security Ordinance (HCSO): employers with 20+ employees must spend $2.56-$3.85/hr per employee on healthcare. Paid Parental Leave: employers with 20+ employees must supplement CA Paid Family Leave to 100% of wages for 8 weeks. Fair Chance Ordinance: cannot ask about criminal history until after conditional offer. Commuter Benefits: employers with 20+ employees must offer pre-tax transit benefits. Lactation accommodation required. Multiple required workplace posters.

All SF businesses must separate waste into 3 streams: recycling, composting, and landfill. Food businesses must use compostable takeout containers (no polystyrene). No free checkout bags (must charge $0.25+). Violations can result in fines. SF Environment offers free technical assistance to set up compliant waste systems.

SF has banned the sale of ALL flavored tobacco products, including menthol cigarettes. E-cigarettes/vapes without FDA premarket approval are also banned. If you're opening a retail store, do NOT plan on selling tobacco/vape products unless you verify current regulations.

sf_specific_gotchas.issue: Spotify/Pandora Not Licensed for Business Use
sf_specific_gotchas.detail: Personal Spotify, Apple Music, Pandora, and similar streaming subscriptions explicitly prohibit commercial/business use in their terms of service. Playing personal streaming in your business is BOTH a TOS violation AND a PRO licensing violation. Use a licensed B2B music service instead.

sf_specific_gotchas.issue: Lease Review — ADA and Accessibility Allocation
sf_specific_gotchas.detail: Before signing a lease, understand who is responsible for ADA compliance — the landlord or tenant. SF's former ABE ordinance required landlords to handle primary entrance accessibility, but the program ended April 2025. Current law still requires compliance but responsibility is often ambiguous. Negotiate this explicitly in your lease. Administrative Code Chapter 38 requires landlords to provide an Access Information Notice to tenants for spaces 7,500 sq ft or less.
